messages.text: [PULSE] REST_MODE_WEEK_TWO: HYP-063 validated 11 days ago, still in rest_mode. Week 2 — decision needed.

[PULSE] OUTCOME_LOOP_STALL: 20 closed OANDA trades but 0 matched to open decisions — the win/loss loop is not closing (CLAUDE.md NON-NEGOTIABLE #2). Oracle is learning from nothing. Check pair/timestamp/system matching in decision_logger.update_outcome.

[PULSE] OUTCOME_LOOP_STALL: 15 closed OANDA trades but 0 matched to open decisions — the win/loss loop is not closing (CLAUDE.md NON-NEGOTIABLE #2). Oracle is learning from nothing. Check pair/timestamp/system matching in decision_logger.update_outcome.

[PULSE] OUTCOME_LOOP_STALL: 15 closed OANDA trades but 0 matched to open decisions — the win/loss loop is not closing (CLAUDE.md NON-NEGOTIABLE #2). Oracle is learning from nothing. Check pair/timestamp/system matching in decision_logger.update_outcome.

[REST-031] The URGENT OUTCOME_LOOP_STALL alarm is a FALSE POSITIVE — the forex loop IS closing. pulse re-scans OANDA's last-100 closed trades every 2h and screams whenever 0 re-match, but 0 re-matches is the healthy steady state (run_oanda_paper closes them at close time; native records show real outcomes: tid29 USD_JPY WIN, tid25/59/119 AUD_NZD LOSS, tid134-165 USD_CAD LOSS). The alarm can't tell 'already closed' from 'never matched.' Two real gaps remain: (1) alarm should fire only on closed trades whose decision record is still OPEN/absent; (2) a few AUD_USD/GBP_USD/EUR_USD fills (tid47/51/72/83/93/105) never close because update_outcome matches on pair+timestamp, not trade_id — and the trade_id join key ALREADY exists in oanda_fills.jsonl + the decision records, just unused (that's REST-030 Change 2; data's already there). Also VERIFIED RQ-REST-018: c7908a9 killed the constant-1.0 commitment_score (July entries now log honest None); real score still unwired = REST-030 Change 1. Net: the URGENT stall is noisy, not on fire. RQ-REST-019b sharpened with both fixes.
